steps.title: Block the circle (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a loose circle at the size you want the ball to be. Don't worry about perfection — this is just a guide.

steps.title: Refine the circle (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, lay a second loose pass over the first if the circle drifted. The goal is to feel out the cleanest path your ink stroke will follow next.

steps.title: Switch to ink and commit to the circle
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Draw one confident circle over your pencil guide in a single pass. One smooth committed line reads as a sphere; many overlapping tries read as a hairy blob.

steps.title: Ink the equator
steps.description: Still in ink, draw a gentle curve across the middle of the ball, curving slightly downward at the ends. That curve tells the viewer the ball is a sphere, not a flat disc.

steps.title: Ink the segment lines
steps.description: Add 2 or 3 curved lines arcing from the top of the ball to the bottom, following the form. Space them unevenly — natural is better than symmetric.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guide lines that distract from the ink. Tidy up anywhere strokes overshoot or don't meet cleanly.
